items.scenario: You are a junior majoring in environmental engineering. Your hydrology professor recently announced that the take-home final will be replaced this semester with an in-person practical lab using equipment you have only used twice. You feel unprepared and want to ask about additional practice time before the exam date.
items.direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the format change has left you concerned about your readiness
Ask whether extra practice sessions or open lab hours could be arranged
Offer to coordinate a small study group if other classmates share your concern
items.subject: Practice time before the practical hydrology final

items.scenario: You started a summer internship at a small civic-tech firm three weeks ago. Your team uses a daily morning standup that runs forty-five minutes, much longer than the standups you have seen elsewhere, and you have noticed that several interns including yourself rarely contribute. Your supervisor invited written feedback before the next planning cycle.
items.direction: Write an email to your supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for inviting feedback this early in the cycle
Describe what you have observed about the current standup format
Suggest a small change you believe would help interns participate more
items.subject: Feedback on the morning standup format

items.scenario: You recently bought a refurbished electric pressure cooker from an online retailer. The unit arrived with a damaged sealing ring and the locking handle does not click into place, which means the cooker cannot reach pressure. The retailer's website asks customers to email the warranty team rather than calling.
items.direction: Write an email to the warranty team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the two issues you found with the pressure cooker
Ask what documentation they need to start the warranty process
Explain why you would prefer a replacement unit rather than a repair
items.subject: Damaged sealing ring and faulty handle on order #HC-31204

items.scenario: You moved into a shared off-campus house last month. The hot water in the upstairs bathroom has stopped working for three mornings in a row, and your landlord asked tenants to email any maintenance issues with details rather than calling. Your roommates have a coding bootcamp final next week and morning showers are essential to their routine.
items.direction: Write an email to your landlord. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe when the hot water issue started and how it has progressed
Explain why the timing is especially difficult for the household this week
Ask when a plumber could come and how she would like to coordinate access
items.subject: Upstairs bathroom hot water issue this week
